title: thelesperma subnudum var. subnudum
intro: Thelesperma subnudum var. subnudum, also known as Naked Marigold, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the Asteraceae family and is recognized by its bright yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. Thelesperma subnudum var. subnudum has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and soothing skin irritations. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the flowers and leaves, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in ointments and salves.
